## Laughing Mahogany Alligator Game Implementation

### Game Description

Laughing Mahogany Alligator is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a grid of squares. Players take turns placing their pieces (alligators) on the board. The goal is to create a line of five alligators in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Simplified Rules Implementation

Here is a simplified implementation of the Laughing Mahogany Alligator game in Ludax:

```ludax
(game "Laughing Mahogany Alligator"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 10 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Piece Movement and Capture

To make the game more interesting, let's add piece movement and capture rules. Players can move their alligators to adjacent empty squares. If a player lands on a square occupied by an opponent's alligator, they can capture it.

```ludax
(game "Laughing Mahogany Alligator"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 10 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Special Alligator Abilities

Let's introduce special abilities for the alligators. Some alligators can jump over other pieces, while others can block opponents' movements.

```ludax
(game "Laughing Mahogany Alligator"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 10 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent occupied))))
                    (effects 
                        (jump (adjacent occupied))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Complete Implementation

Here is the complete implementation of the Laughing Mahogany Alligator game:

```ludax
(game "Laughing Mahogany Alligator"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 10 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (9 9))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent occupied))))
                    (effects 
                        (jump (adjacent occupied))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

This implementation provides a basic structure for the Laughing Mahogany Alligator game. You can modify and extend the rules to create a more complex and engaging game.

# Cordial Stirring Teal Game Implementation

## Game Description

Cordial Stirring Teal is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. The goal is to create a connected line of five pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

## Game Rules

### Equipment

The game is played on a 5x5 square board.

### Start Rules

The game starts with an empty board. Player 1 places their first piece on the center square.

### Play Rules

Players alternate turns. On each turn, a player can place one piece on an empty square.

### End Rules

The game ends when a player creates a connected line of five pieces in any direction. That player wins. If the board is full and no player has won, the game is a draw.

## Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Cordial Stirring Teal"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Incremental Additions

### Addition 1: Capturing Pieces

Players can capture their opponent's pieces by surrounding them.

```lark
(game "Cordial Stirring Teal"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Addition 2: Scoring

Players earn points for capturing their opponent's pieces.

```lark
(game "Cordial Stirring Teal"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Addition 3: Forced Pass

Players are forced to pass if they have no valid moves.

```lark
(game "Cordial Stirring Teal"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

# Arboreal Warm Trogon Game Implementation

## Game Description

Arboreal Warm Trogon is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a grid of squares. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board, trying to create a connected line of pieces.

## Game Rules

### Equipment

The game is played on a rectangular board with 8x8 squares.

### Start Rules

The game starts with an empty board.

### Play Rules

Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. A player can only place a piece on an empty square.

### End Rules

The game ends when one player has a connected line of 5 pieces. That player wins.

## Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Arboreal Warm Trogon"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 8 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Incremental Rule Additions

### Adding a Capture Mechanic

Players can capture an opponent's piece by placing their own piece adjacent to it.

```ludax
(game "Arboreal Warm Trogon"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 8 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding a Scoring System

Players earn points for capturing opponent's pieces.

```ludax
(game "Arboreal Warm Trogon"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 8 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding a Forced Pass Mechanic

Players are forced to pass if they have no valid moves.

```ludax
(game "Arboreal Warm Trogon"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 8 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (passed both) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Game Description: Authentic Military Hyena

Authentic Military Hyena is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a set of rules that allow players to place and capture pieces.

## Game Rules

### Game Equipment

The game is played on a rectangular board of size 5x5.

### Game Start

The game starts with an empty board.

### Game Play

Players take turns placing pieces on empty squares. A player can place a piece on an empty square. If a player places a piece adjacent to an opponent's piece, they can capture that piece.

### Game End

The game ends when one player has no more pieces on the board or when the board is full. The player with the most pieces on the board wins.

## Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Authentic Military Hyena"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (and (not (exists (occupied mover))) (full_board)) (draw))
            (if (> (count (occupied mover)) (count (occupied opponent))) (mover win))
            (if (< (count (occupied mover)) (count (occupied opponent))) (opponent win))
        )
    )
)
```

## Explanation

*   The game starts with an empty board.
*   Players take turns placing pieces on empty squares.
*   A player can capture an opponent's piece by placing a piece adjacent to it.
*   The game ends when one player has no more pieces on the board or when the board is full.
*   The player with the most pieces on the board wins.

## Incremental Rules

### Rule 1: Limit the number of pieces a player can place

We can add a rule to limit the number of pieces a player can place.

```ludax
(game "Authentic Military Hyena"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
        )
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (and (not (exists (occupied mover))) (full_board)) (draw))
            (if (> (count (occupied mover)) (count (occupied opponent))) (mover win))
            (if (< (count (occupied mover)) (count (occupied opponent))) (opponent win))
        )
    )
)
```

### Rule 2: Introduce a "hyena" piece that can move and capture

We can introduce a "hyena" piece that can move and capture.

```ludax
(game "Authentic Military Hyena"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (move 
                    (destination (adjacent occupied mover))
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (and (not (exists (occupied mover))) (full_board)) (draw))
            (if (> (count (occupied mover)) (count (occupied opponent))) (mover win))
            (if (< (count (occupied mover)) (count (occupied opponent))) (opponent win))
        )
    )
)
```

## Cheerful Smiling Sponge Game Implementation

The Cheerful Smiling Sponge game is a novel two-player abstract strategy game. Here's a simplified version of the game implemented in Ludax.

### Game Description

The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their sponges on empty cells. The goal is to create a line of three or more sponges in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally). The game ends when a player achieves this goal or when the board is full.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Cheerful Smiling Sponge"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Incremental Rule Additions

#### 1. Sponge Capture

Players can capture their opponent's sponges by placing their own sponge adjacent to it.

```ludax
(game "Cheerful Smiling Sponge"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

#### 2. Scoring

Players earn points for capturing their opponent's sponges.

```ludax
(game "Cheerful Smiling Sponge"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

#### 3. Special Power-Ups

Introduce special power-ups that players can collect to gain advantages.

```ludax
(game "Cheerful Smiling Sponge"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
        (power_ups (list "swap" "block"))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (1 1))
            (place P2 (4 4))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (if (has_power_up mover "swap") 
                    (swap mover opponent))
                (if (has_power_up mover "block") 
                    (block mover))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (by_score))    
        )
    )
)
```

This is a basic implementation of the Cheerful Smiling Sponge game in Ludax. You can add more rules and features to make the game more complex and interesting.

## Game Description: Nano Leech Of Perspective

Nano Leech Of Perspective is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their pieces (leech) on the board. The goal is to create a line of three or more leeches in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

## Game Rules

### Game Equipment

The game is played on a 5x5 square board.

### Game Start

The game starts with an empty board.

### Game Play

Players take turns placing their leeches on the board. A player can only place a leech on an empty square.

### Game End

The game ends when a player creates a line of three or more leeches in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally). The player with the line wins the game. If the board is full and no player has won, the game is a draw.

## Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Nano Leech Of Perspective"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Incremental Addition of Rules

### Adding Leech Movement

In this version, leeches can move one square in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) after they have been placed.

```lark
(game "Nano Leech Of Perspective"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
                (place 
                    (destination 
                        (and 
                            (adjacent empty) 
                            (not (adjacent (prev_move mover)))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Leech Capture

In this version, a leech can capture an opponent's leech by moving to the square occupied by the opponent's leech.

```lark
(game "Nano Leech Of Perspective"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
                (place 
                    (destination 
                        (or 
                            (and 
                                (adjacent empty) 
                                (not (adjacent (prev_move mover)))
                            )
                            (occupied opponent)
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move opponent)))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Complete Game

The complete game with all the rules.

```lark
(game "Nano Leech Of Perspective"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
                (place 
                    (destination 
                        (or 
                            (and 
                                (adjacent empty) 
                                (not (adjacent (prev_move mover)))
                            )
                            (occupied opponent)
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move opponent)))
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

## Turtle Of Pastoral Luxury

### Game Description

Turtle Of Pastoral Luxury is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board of size 5x5. Players take turns placing turtles on empty spaces. The goal is to create a connected group of turtles that spans from one edge of the board to the opposite edge.

### Simplified Rules

The simplified rules for Turtle Of Pastoral Luxury are as follows:

* The game is played on a 5x5 square board.
* Players take turns placing turtles on empty spaces.
* A player wins if they have a connected group of turtles that spans from one edge of the board to the opposite edge.

### Ludax Implementation

Here is a simplified implementation of Turtle Of Pastoral Luxury in Ludax:

```ludax
(game "Turtle Of Pastoral Luxury"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or 
                    (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 1) 
                    (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 1)
                ) 
                (mover win)
            )
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding More Rules

Let's add more rules to the game. We want to introduce a new effect that allows a player to capture an opponent's turtle if it is adjacent to their own turtle.

```ludax
(game "Turtle Of Pastoral Luxury"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or 
                    (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 1) 
                    (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 1)
                ) 
                (mover win)
            )
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Start Rules

Let's add start rules to the game. We want to place a turtle on the center of the board for each player.

```ludax
(game "Turtle Of Pastoral Luxury"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
            (place P2 (3 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or 
                    (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 1) 
                    (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 1)
                ) 
                (mover win)
            )
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Rendering

Let's add rendering to the game. We want to color the turtles for each player.

```ludax
(game "Turtle Of Pastoral Luxury"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))
            (place P2 (3 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or 
                    (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 1) 
                    (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 1)
                ) 
                (mover win)
            )
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

## Flawless Boa Of Aptitude Game Implementation

The Flawless Boa Of Aptitude is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 8x8. The objective of the game is to create a "boa" by placing and capturing pieces.

### Game Rules

1. The game starts with an empty board.
2. Player 1 (P1) places their piece on a random empty square.
3. Player 2 (P2) places their piece on a random empty square.
4. Players alternate turns.
5. On each turn, a player can place a piece on an empty square adjacent to one of their existing pieces.
6. If a player captures an opponent's piece, they can place their piece on the captured square.
7. The game ends when one player creates a "boa" (a line of 5 or more pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).
8. The player with the "boa" wins the game.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Flawless Boa Of Aptitude"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (4 4))
            (place P2 (4 5))
        )
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination 
                        (and 
                            empty 
                            (adjacent 
                                (or 
                                    (occupied mover) 
                                    (occupied opponent)
                                )
                            )
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (capture 
                            (adjacent 
                                (occupied opponent)
                            ) 
                            increment_score:true
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if 
                (or 
                    (line 5) 
                    (line 5 orientation:vertical) 
                    (line 5 orientation:diagonal) 
                    (line 5 orientation:back_diagonal)
                ) 
                (mover win)
            )
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation

The game implementation consists of the following parts:

*   The game starts with an empty 8x8 board and two players, P1 and P2.
*   The `start` rule places the first two pieces on the board at positions (4, 4) and (4, 5).
*   The `play` rule allows players to take turns placing pieces on the board. The `once_through` phase allows P1 to place their first piece, and the `repeat` phase allows both players to take turns.
*   The `place` action allows a player to place a piece on an empty square adjacent to one of their existing pieces or an opponent's piece.
*   The `effects` section allows a player to capture an opponent's piece when placing a new piece.
*   The `end` rule checks if a player has created a "boa" (a line of 5 or more pieces in a row) and declares them the winner. If the board is full and no player has won, the game ends in a draw.

### Future Enhancements

To make the game more challenging and interesting, you can add additional rules, such as:

*   Allowing players to move existing pieces instead of just placing new ones.
*   Introducing special pieces with unique abilities.
*   Creating a scoring system to reward players for capturing opponent pieces.
*   Implementing a timer to limit the time players have to make their moves.

These enhancements can be implemented by adding new rules, actions, and effects to the game implementation.

prompt:
Invent simple rules for a novel two player abstract strategy game called Daffy Uncovered Cuscus. Implement it in the ludax language. You will find attached the ludax's grammar as well as a few examples of games implemented in ludax. Start by implementing a simplified version of your rules, and then incrementally add rules that are harder to express in ludax. At each step, make sure you write a compilable game according to ludax's grammar.

        grammar.lark:
// ---Root---
game: "(" "game" name players equipment rules rendering? ")"

// ---Players---
players: "(" "players" positive_int ")"

// ---Equipment---
equipment: "(" "equipment" board ")"
board: "(" "board" (board_square | board_rectangle | board_hexagon | board_hex_rectangle) ")"
board_square: "(" "square" number ")"
board_rectangle: "(" "rectangle" number number ")"
board_hexagon: "(" "hexagon"  number ")"
board_hex_rectangle: "(" "hex_rectangle" number number ")"

// ---Rules---
rules: "(" "rules" start_rules? play_rules end_rules ")"

// ---Start rules---
start_rules: "(" "start" start_rule+ ")"
start_rule: start_place
start_place: "(" "place" player_reference pattern_arg ")"

// ---Play rules---
play_rules: "(" "play" play_phase+ ")"
play_phase: phase_once_through | phase_repeat
phase_once_through: "(" "once_through" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
phase_repeat: "(" "repeat" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
play_mover_order: "(" player_reference+ ")"

play_mechanic: play_place force_pass? 
play_place: "(" "place" mover_reference? place_destination_constraint place_result_constraint? play_effects?")"
force_pass: "(" "force_pass" ")"

// ---Constraints---
place_destination_constraint: "(" "destination" super_mask ")"
place_result_constraint: "(" "result" super_predicate ")"

// ---Effects---
play_effects: "(" "effects" play_effect+ ")"
play_effect: effect_capture 
           | effect_flip
           | effect_increment_score
           | effect_set_score

effect_capture: "(" "capture" super_mask mover_reference? increment_score_arg? ")"
effect_flip: "(" "flip" super_mask mover_reference? ")"
effect_increment_score: "(" "increment_score" mover_reference function ")"
effect_set_score: "(" "set_score" mover_reference function ")"

// ---Functions---
function: function_add
        | function_connected
        | function_constant
        | function_count
        | function_line
        | function_multiply
        | function_score
        | function_subtract

function_add: "(" "add" function+ ")"
function_connected: "(" "connected" multi_mask_arg mover_reference? direction_arg? ")"
function_constant: positive_int
function_count: "(" "count" super_mask ")"
function_line: "(" "line" positive_int orientation_arg? exact_arg? ")"
function_multiply: "(" "multiply" function+ ")"
function_score: "(" "score" mover_reference ")"
function_subtract: "(" "subtract" function function ")"

// ---End rules---
end_rules: "(" "end" end_rule+ ")"
end_rule: "(" "if" super_predicate end_rule_result ")"
?end_rule_result: result_win | result_lose | result_draw | result_by_score

// -- Result definitions --
result_win: "(" mover_reference "win" ")"
result_lose: "(" mover_reference "lose" ")"
result_draw: "(" "draw" ")"
result_by_score: "(" "by_score" ")"

// -- Mask definitions --
super_mask: mask | super_mask_and | super_mask_or | super_mask_not
super_mask_and: "(" "and" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_or: "(" "or" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_not: "(" "not" super_mask ")"

mask: mask_adjacent
    | mask_center
    | mask_corners
    | mask_custodial
    | mask_edge
    | mask_empty
    | mask_occupied
    | mask_pattern
    | mask_prev_move

mask_adjacent: "(" "adjacent" super_mask direction_arg? ")"
mask_center: "center"
mask_corners: "corners"
mask_custodial: "(" "custodial" custodial_length_arg mover_reference? orientation_arg? ")"
mask_edge: "(" "edge" edge ")"
mask_empty: "empty"
mask_occupied: "occupied" | "(" "occupied" mover_reference ")"
mask_pattern: "(" "pattern" dimensions_arg pattern_arg rotate_arg? ")"
mask_prev_move: "(" "prev_move" mover_reference ")"

// "Multi-masks" are special keywords that are manually split into multiple
// sub-masks at compile time. This is mostly useful for the "connected" function,
// which expects a list of masks to check for connections between
multi_mask: multi_mask_corners
          | multi_mask_edges
          | multi_mask_edges_no_corners

multi_mask_corners: "corners"
multi_mask_edges: "edges"
multi_mask_edges_no_corners: "edgesNoCorners"

// ---Predicate definitions---
super_predicate: predicate | super_predicate_and | super_predicate_or | super_predicate_not
super_predicate_and: "(" "and" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_or: "(" "or" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_not: "(" "not" super_predicate ")"

predicate: predicate_equals
         | predicate_exists
         | predicate_full_board
         | predicate_function
         | predicate_greater_equals
         | predicate_less_equals
         | predicate_mover_is
         | predicate_passed

predicate_equals: "(" "=" function+ ")"
predicate_exists: "(" "exists" super_mask ")" // technically equivalent to (>= (count mask) 1) 
predicate_full_board: "(" "full_board" ")"
predicate_function: function // special syntax which is equivalent to "(>= function 1)"
predicate_greater_equals: "(" ">=" function function ")"
predicate_less_equals: "(" "<=" function function ")"
predicate_mover_is: "(" "mover_is" player_reference ")"
predicate_passed: "(" "passed" (mover_reference | BOTH) ")"

// Additional (potentially optional) arguments for predicates
custodial_length_arg: ANY | positive_int
dimensions_arg: "(" positive_int positive_int ")"
direction_arg: "direction:" direction
exact_arg: "exact:" boolean
increment_score_arg: "increment_score:" boolean
multi_mask_arg: multi_mask | "(" super_mask+ ")"
orientation_arg: "orientation:" orientation
pattern_arg: "(" positive_int+ ")"
rotate_arg: "rotate:" boolean

// Optional rendering details
rendering: "(" "rendering" rendering_detail+ ")"
rendering_detail: color_assignment

color_assignment: "(" "color" player_reference color ")"

// General-purpose definitions
?number: SIGNED_NUMBER
?positive_int: /[0-9]+/
?boolean: TRUE | FALSE
?edge: TOP | BOTTOM | LEFT | RIGHT | TOP_LEFT | TOP_RIGHT | BOTTOM_LEFT | BOTTOM_RIGHT
?direction: UP | DOWN | LEFT | RIGHT | UP_LEFT | UP_RIGHT | DOWN_LEFT | DOWN_RIGHT | VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?orientation: VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?color: WHITE | BLACK
// ----------------------------

?player_reference: P1 | P2
?mover_reference: MOVER | OPPONENT
name: STRING
variable_name: /\?[a-z][a-z0-9]*/
id: /[a-zA-Z0-9_]+/

// Constants
TOP: "top"
BOTTOM: "bottom"
UP: "up"
DOWN: "down"
LEFT: "left"
RIGHT: "right"
TOP_LEFT: "top_left"
TOP_RIGHT: "top_right"
BOTTOM_LEFT: "bottom_left"
BOTTOM_RIGHT: "bottom_right"
UP_LEFT: "up_left"
UP_RIGHT: "up_right"
DOWN_LEFT: "down_left"
DOWN_RIGHT: "down_right"
VERTICAL: "vertical"
HORIZONTAL: "horizontal"
ORTHOGONAL: "orthogonal"
DIAGONAL: "diagonal"
BACK_DIAGONAL: "back_diagonal"
FORWARD_DIAGONAL: "forward_diagonal"
ANY: "any"
TRUE: "true"
FALSE: "false"
MOVER: "mover"
OPPONENT: "opponent"
P1: "P1"
P2: "P2"
BOTH: "both"
WHITE: "white"
BLACK: "black"

// Lark imports
%import common.ESCAPED_STRING -> STRING
%import common.SIGNED_NUMBER
%import common.WS
%ignore WS


        example games:
        

(game "Complexity Demo" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Four"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 6 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination (and empty (or (edge bottom) (adjacent occupied direction:up)))))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Six"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 6) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Hex"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hex_rectangle 11 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 2) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 2) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Gomoku" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 15))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5 exact:true) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Pente" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination center))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Reversi" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (28 35))
            (place P2 (27 36))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (exists
                            (custodial any)
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)


(game "Tic-Tac-Toe" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 3))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalath" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hexagon 9))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play 
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover lose))
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalax" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 13))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (and 
                            (not (line 5))
                            (not (= (line 4) 1))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 4) 2) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
